column1: GN[Tournament Game: Beat the Kyu, Be a Dan 2024 (103535) R:1 (apetresc vs heavenlyalchemy)]
C[MorwenEdhelwen: I feel like it's become tradition for us to play a game when I come back to playing go for a bit :)
C[apetresc: Ooooh, I didn’t recognize/remember the username! Heyyy long time no talk :) How’re you doing?
MorwenEdhelwen: Yeah. I changed up my username. ""Maaya"" is a bit too public facing to feel like I can do fun stuff with online anymore ^^
MorwenEdhelwen: I'm doing good :) I'm sure it will hit me one day that I'm actually an adult, but I'm still waiting on that to feel real
MorwenEdhelwen: (I say as if I'm not been an adult for 18 years...)
C[apetresc: It's a Tolkien reference right? I didn't know you were into Tolkien :D
apetresc: Haha, so glad to hear you're doing well. I'm alright too, also taking very long hiatuses from go
apetresc: Whereabouts are you living these days? Do I remember correctly that you were up north for a while?
C[MorwenEdhelwen: Yep. It is a Tolkien reference. My partner picked it out for me :) they are a big Tolkien fan
MorwenEdhelwen: I'm still out in Saskatoon. Came here for school
MorwenEdhelwen: Stayed because the cost of living is so much higher everywhere else :p
MorwenEdhelwen: Honestly it's a beautiful city and I like it a lot more than Windsor, but it is in the middle of nowhere >.<
MorwenEdhelwen: Last I heard, you were out in the GTA. Still loving that big city life?
apetresc: Nooo I never liked the big city life ^^
column2: I moved to Waterloo a few years back

column1: apetresc: Saskatoon sounds lovely :D What'd you study there?
C[MorwenEdhelwen: Oh, my brother lives out there. I've been through KW a couple times, but I've never had the chance to stay long
MorwenEdhelwen: I hope you are enjoying it there :)
MorwenEdhelwen: I went to grad school for psychology, and ended up running my own business to stay in the field, so that's neat
MorwenEdhelwen: I imagine there are a fair number of Massey grads out in the KW area. Do you run into many anymore?
C[apetresc: Nope, none of the Massey folk who went to UW stayed in the area. They’re mostly in Toronto and California now, as far as the ones I know of
C[MorwenEdhelwen: Sorry I'm not chatty. I'm away from home at the moment. I will be back on Wednesday:)
C[MorwenEdhelwen: I'm back from the Manitoba wilderness now!

MorwenEdhelwen: I haven't followed Massey people all that closely over the years. I have run in to Yifan out here in Saskatoon, and we met for bubble tea before the pandemic. That was nice, but otherwise I don't know where anyone dispersed to really
C[apetresc: Yeah, same. I'm really bad at keeping up with people
C[MorwenEdhelwen: Yeah, I'm the same way.
MorwenEdhelwen: I forget, how old are your little ones now? Your oldest kiddo must getting up to school age, I'd have thought. How does THAT feel? :)
apetresc: Yup! The older one's in grade 2, the younger in SK, starting grade school in the fall

MorwenEdhelwen: I remember you sharing on Facebook about your first. I can't believe that's been 7 years already. Then again, I've been in this I job now for 6 or 7 myself, so I guess I shouldn't be surprised
MorwenEdhelwen: Time is just wonky I guess
MorwenEdhelwen: Thanks for the game, Adrian :) This game was a lot more reading heavy than the last few that I've been playing, it made for a nice change
MorwenEdhelwen: Definitely. Let me send you a challenge
MorwenEdhelwen: Assuming you're okay with the settings of this one. Let me know if you'd prefer different time settings or anything :)
C[Nissa: Hi, have a good game 😁
C[apetresc: Is your name an MtG reference, or just a coincidence? :)
C[Nissa: it is actually! Nissa Revane is my favourite MtG character
C[apetresc: Nice! I love MtG too :)
C[apetresc: What formats do you play?
C[Nissa: Unfortunately I haven't been able to play for a long time, I moved a few years ago and haven't met anyone where I live now that plays it.
Nissa: But I used to really like playing with the commanders. And I had a group of friends with whom we'd always play booster draft.
C[apetresc: I play pretty much every format except Commander, but I especially like booster drafts and cube drafts :)
C[Nissa: Ah really? Why do you not play Commander? Do you not like it?
C[apetresc: Oh I bet I would like it, but I feel like Commander works better when you have a consistent group of people that you know
apetresc: I don't really have a playgroup, I just play with randoms at tournaments and online and stuff
apetresc: So competitive 1v1 formats work better
Nissa: Yeah that makes sense, tbh I've completely stopped playing since I've moved to another city, as it feels kind of weird to me to play with random people. But I probably should, I do miss it.
GN[Tournament Game: Rubikrew Monthly #1 (32905) R:1 (bwhitz vs apetresc)]
C[bwhitz: Awesome so far, mostly Mechanical Eng courses this term but still good! Its been really cool reading about Rubikloud online!
C[bwhitz: :( Should of thought ahead a few more moves on that last one
C[apetresc: Actually I think the K3/L3 sequence was very good - it made the cut at K5 much more dangerous. The mistake IMO was not taking K5 instead of E13
apetresc: (Since it let me protect K5 with K6)
C[bwhitz: Thanks for the advice, the power of 2 diagonal pieces is huge
C[bwhitz: thanks, learn something new everyday
C[bwhitz: Im not the best at determining when the game is over, but I sure dont have many good places to go anymore

C[apetresc: Yeah, we've entered the late endgame stage now, we're basically haggling over the the last few boundary points. Advanced players can basically count and estimate the final score to within +/- 2 points at this point, so if the margin is any bigger than that they'll often just resign here, but for anyone below, say, 6-dan, it's definitely still useful to play it out all the way to the end.
C[apetresc: There's still this and E14, and *then* the game is over :)
C[bwhitz: I have so many games on the go (no pun intended), just got to keep practicing
bwhitz: Thanks for all the help

C[BenKyoTutoring: To be honest I still don't know which side to attach onto lol. C[BenKyoTutoring: I used to play ultra-calm a lot, like...

BenKyoTutoring: But these days I'm more active, aggressive and balanced (eg. fewer low moves, usually) than I used to be, or at least I like to think so. I started using AI a bit recently for game reviews, finally, and something I've gleaned from it is...well, I guess a greater sense of sente, urgency, severity. For situations like this even (variation posted above), I may consider more strongly to play R8 or something just so I'm developing the board more and worrying less about cash. But really, because O3 turns into this situation of S4 being a respectable choice, and it's a slow second-line move, better to prevent in the first place!
BenKyoTutoring: Hmm, also double approach is not so punishing as people used to think. I always felt that way
column2: I never liked my result that much after I double approached people, and now AI tells us ""yes, it's fine to let those happen as long as you're getting something really big instead""

column1: BenKyoTutoring: To be honest though, that variation is probably still fine for white and I'm not sure how AI feels about it, but I know that there are a few cases of S4 that AI didn't like from me. Ultimately, it's a second-line move!

C[BenKyoTutoring: I don't have a lot of experience with Q9 in this position so I'm not sure what's best, but it should be fine just to settle here, especially if it's in sente or leaving good follow-ups. First thought was R5, but I also considered Q6, which ultimately just feels wrong here. I don't have anything too helpful to say here except that white should do some combination of getting a base and fixing the cut, all the while looking for a chance to pincer black's bottom group. I'm also trying to make sure things will be okay efficiency-wise with my cut situation at F4, but at least it's not something I need to rush back to, as I can just F5 if black plays F3 and that shouldn't be particularly bad for me in this board state.
BenKyoTutoring: Since Q9 is a bit close to R6, kicking shouldn't be too bad.
BenKyoTutoring: The variation above is usually something white needs to avoid, though here it might be okay

C[BenKyoTutoring: Tempted to J3 since I can also attack the black to the right, but my white corner is not completely invulnerable so I guess for now I will approach one of the 3-4 corners. I don't know which is better, approach upper right to hamper black's development there (which has begun with Q9), or approach upper left to build my left-side potential and complement F5.
BenKyoTutoring: I suspect the latter is best, it looks spacious.
BenKyoTutoring: If black develops the top and by extension the upper right quadrant, invading the right would work well because Q9 is still a little bit weak
C[BenKyoTutoring: I usually like to approach low these days, but making sure I'm high is a good way to complement F5 I think.
BenKyoTutoring: With 2 corners and komi, I can afford to let black have some cash here if we're talking about balance.
C[BenKyoTutoring: Interesting, don't see this often.

C[BenKyoTutoring: I decided to ignore the upper left because black's thinness there with that last move should leave me many options even if black adds one or even two more moves. If black does something simple like attach under at C15, or just C16, when I block to develop the left side, black ends up having a big shape defect and needing to add a stone around E16, so black might end up inefficient or at least slow.
BenKyoTutoring: Er, yeah pretty much just that one
C[BenKyoTutoring: I don't know yet whether I want to press the bottom with H4 or invade, so for now I'm playing nothing there

BenKyoTutoring: I have B18 in the upper left to try to get a small life locally, or the crosscut at D18, which I'd prefer to do only after I've done some other exchanges on the top black territorial formation, since it makes black stronger when I crosscut and black captures
BenKyoTutoring: So for now I'm going to do some forcing stuff on the top instead of just running out the upper left white group right away. I also want E10, but I think it can wait

BenKyoTutoring: If black makes me add a stone to take the left territory, I should be happy, and if black invades, black will end up with multiple weak groups near each other, so I doubt black will invade
BenKyoTutoring: Thus I don't have to worry about the left for now
BenKyoTutoring: I also want to attach at Q10, but it's only clearly great for me if black plays like...
BenKyoTutoring: Since I have both ladders. Since black will play in some other way, I'd rather not touch that for now and resolve the top/upper left first

BenKyoTutoring: If black were to add a move to the right side to take this attach away from me, it'll probably be slow for black and I won't really mind anyway, so again I have a reason to stay my hand.
BenKyoTutoring: Laser focus on the top/upper left!
C[BenKyoTutoring: I thought about many moves...
BenKyoTutoring: But I didn't really like any of the variations, so I went with C (J16) because I'm confident I can get black to respond and I can see what black does
column2: either it will give me a way to link up to D15, or it'll be some beneficial forcing moves / kikashi to play before I must switch to the upper left with something like I, K or F in the variation above (C16, D18 or F15)

column1: BenKyoTutoring: I don't think I'll end up choosing B18, as I'd like to keep black's C13 group separated if possible. BenKyoTutoring: But the newer me is a bit more active and aggressive I suppose
BenKyoTutoring: Keeping black separated indirectly protects me left-side potential a bit.
BenKyoTutoring: I should probably comment about the upper right result - it's definitely good for white

BenKyoTutoring: I don't think the result for black on the bottom is particularly good, although I do think black played many correct moves there, and then the upper right is probably a loss of at least... 15-20% I imagine. BenKyoTutoring: I don't blame black for playing that way though with R15, I only knew the better sequence from stuff I studied when I was in Korea and got tested on haha
